title: Broadcast and Event System
description: Sending broadcasts to clients, replaying saved broadcasts, and inter-service messaging

use_cases.name: Send Broadcast to All Open Apps
use_cases.description: Call sendBroadcast(name, data) to postMessage to all .window-app-iframe[data-appUsesSDK=true] iframes

use_cases.name: Save Broadcast for Future App Instances
use_cases.description: When sendToNewAppInstances=true, store broadcast in #broadcastsToSendToNewAppInstances Map for later replay

use_cases.name: Replay Saved Broadcasts to New App
use_cases.description: On new app launch, sendSavedBroadcastsTo(appInstanceID) locates the iframe by UUID and replays all stored broadcasts via postMessage

use_cases.name: Receive and Emit Inbound Webhook Event
use_cases.description: POST /broadcast/webhook with HMAC-signed payload; backend validates timestamp, nonce, signature, then emits events via EventService

use_cases.name: Forward Outbound Event to Peer via Webhook
use_cases.description: Backend listens to outer.* events; enqueues them, flushes batched events to webhook peers via HMAC-signed POST requests

use_cases.name: Publish Events to Redis Pub/Sub
use_cases.description: Mirror outer.pub events to Redis channel 'broadcast.webhook.events' so same-cluster replicas receive them

use_cases.name: Consume Redis Pub/Sub Events
use_cases.description: Subscribe to Redis channel; on message from other instances (different sourceId), filter and emit events locally via EventService

use_cases.name: Register and Initialize Service
use_cases.description: ServiceManager.register(name, factory) creates service instance, resolves dependencies, then calls init(). Services await deps via wait_for_init()

use_cases.name: Retrieve Running Service
use_cases.description: services.get(name) returns service instance only if status=Running; services.aget(name) waits for init before returning

use_cases.name: Subscribe to Event
use_cases.description: EmitterFeature installs .on(selector, callback) and .emit(key, data, meta) on any class instance. Supports wildcard selectors (e.g. outer.*)

use_cases.name: Emit Event to Listeners
use_cases.description: instance.emit(key, data, meta) resolves wildcard + exact-match listeners, invokes concurrently, awaits all promises

use_cases.name: Install BroadcastService via Module
use_cases.description: BroadcastModule.install(context) retrieves services container and registers 'broadcast' service

key_relationships: EmitterFeature is a mixin installed on any AdvancedBase instance
ChannelFeature provides Channel (EventEmitter-like) objects named in CHANNELS static array
key_data_flows: outer.* event fired → outBroadcastEventHandler → Redis publish + webhook queue
Webhook POST received → HMAC validate → Redis mirror + EventService.emit(key, data, {from_outside:true})
Redis message received → filter outer.pub → EventService.emit(key, data, {from_outside:true})
New app launch → sendSavedBroadcastsTo(appInstanceID) → iframe.postMessage per saved broadcast
ServiceManager.register → factory.create → maybe_init_ → init_service_ → status=Running → notify listeners
security_notes: Webhook validation uses HMAC-SHA256 signature over 'timestamp.nonce.rawBody', replay window 300s, per-peer nonce tracking to prevent replay attacks
important_notes: Backend BroadcastService deduplicates outbound events by JSON key before flushing (configurable flush interval, default 5000ms). GUI BroadcastService uses jQuery selectors for iframe targeting. EmitterFeature supports concurrent async listeners with wildcard matching.
